_note: Quality tier = regression-criticality, INDEPENDENT of public listing (stage in labsCatalog.manifest.json). tier = f(usage, owner criticality, lifecycle) — see docs/APP_QUALITY_TIERS.md. Seed values below come from the owner's stated usage; refresh `usage` via scripts/fetch-app-usage.mjs (GA4) and adjust `criticality`/`tier` by hand. A human confirms tier changes; a quiet week does not auto-demote a critical app.

tiers.protected: Do not regress. Full rigor: labs-qa-review + labs-ux-review on major changes, blocking visual/e2e, guardrails required, no sweeping rewrite without an architecture pass.
tiers.standard: Normal rigor: scoped tests, the labs-local-review trio at merge, gates by usual right-sizing.
tiers.experimental: Move fast: sweeping changes fine with minimal testing; skip heavy gates; promote when usage rises or it graduates to stable.

drums.why: Darbuka Rhythm Trainer — used regularly by the owner (stated). Public-stable.

encore.why: Nearly all of the owner's singing practice runs through it. Unlisted because it is PRIVATE (restricted Drive login: owner + husband), not because it is early. Canonical tier != stage example.

count.why: Metronome/count-in used regularly (stated). CONFIRM this is the 'metronome app' the owner meant.

words.why: Public-stable rhythm app; default standard pending GA.

stanza.why: Practice viewer; default standard pending GA. Raise if usage is high.

melodia.why: Early development, low usage (stated) — sweeping changes are low-risk.

sight.why: Color Sight Trainer — early development, low usage (stated).

muscle.why: Muscle Memory — early development, low usage (stated).

story.why: Generator in development; confirm with GA.

cats.why: Game in development; low-risk. Confirm with GA.
